name: Authorization and/or supervision
justification: Engineering leads supervise ePHI-adjacent work; access reviewed weekly

name: Workforce clearance procedure
justification: Background check and role review at hire

name: Termination procedures
justification: Same-day deprovisioning runbook through the IdP

name: Access authorization
justification: Role-based grants in the IdP; documented approval flow

name: Access establishment and modification
justification: Quarterly access recertification; changes ticketed

name: Security reminders
justification: Monthly security-reminder posts in company chat

name: Protection from malicious software
justification: Managed EDR on all endpoints

name: Log-in monitoring
alternative: IdP anomalous-login alerting reviewed weekly; centralized SSO makes per-application log-in review redundant
justification: Per-application log-in monitoring is not reasonable and appropriate for a 40-person single-sign-on environment

name: Password management
alternative: Passwordless WebAuthn with IdP-enforced MFA replaces password creation/rotation procedures
justification: Password management procedures are superseded by passwordless authentication

name: Testing and revision procedures
justification: Annual disaster-recovery tabletop plus restore test

name: Applications and data criticality analysis
justification: Criticality ranking maintained in the DR plan

name: Contingency operations
justification: Fully remote organization; no facility houses ePHI systems. Facility-level contingency operations are the cloud provider's responsibility, evidenced by the provider's SOC 2 report

name: Facility security plan
justification: Fully remote organization; no facility houses ePHI systems. Workstation protections are addressed under 164.310(b)-(c)

name: Access control and validation procedures
justification: No facility access to control or validate; provider data-center controls inherited and reviewed annually

name: Maintenance records
justification: No organization-controlled facility; provider maintenance records are covered by the provider's SOC 2 report

name: Accountability
justification: MDM asset register tracks every endpoint

name: Data backup and storage
alternative: Zero-ePHI-on-endpoints policy enforced by MDM and DLP; nothing to copy before moving equipment
justification: Pre-movement backup is not reasonable and appropriate because endpoints are barred from storing ePHI; the equivalent measure is the enforced zero-local-storage policy

name: Automatic logoff
justification: 15-minute idle session timeout enforced at the IdP and in the application

name: Encryption and decryption
justification: Encryption at rest: AES-256 via cloud KMS on every datastore holding ePHI — reasonable and appropriate given cloud hosting and 12 covered-entity customers

name: Mechanism to authenticate electronic protected health information
justification: Object versioning plus checksum validation on ePHI stores

name: Integrity controls
justification: TLS with integrity protection on all service-to-service traffic

name: Encryption
justification: TLS 1.2+ enforced on every external connection carrying ePHI
